content.content:
1	{
2	  "body": "Uberon has:

[foramen secundum](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0006678) [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) [atrial septum secundum](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004155)

NCIT has:

[Septum Secundum](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_C34295) [Anatomic_Structure_Is_Physical_Part_Of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_R82) [Foramen Secundum](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_C34179)

The 'part of' relationship goes in the opposite direction.

Uberon contains mappings to the respective NCIT terms. Which is correct?",
3	  "comments": [
4	    {
5	      "id": "IC_kwDOAAidd86sXUwY",
6	      "author": {
7	        "login": "rays22"
8	      },
9	      "authorAssociation": "COLLABORATOR",
10	      "body": "Good catch.
I am afraid neither the current Uberon nor the NCIT 'part of' relationship is correct.

1. Uberon [UBERON:0006678 foramen secundum](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/uberon/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FUBERON_0006678) has:
    * ~'foramen secundum part of atrial septum secundum'~ --> **FALSE!!**
        * CORRECTLY: [UBERON:0006678 foramen secundum](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/uberon/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FUBERON_0006678) **'part of' some 'atrial septum primum'**
            * The corrected definition is based on the description "*the foramen secundum or ostium secundum is a foramen in the septum primum*" in the wikipedia article on [Foramen secundum](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foramen_secundum).
            * The corrected definition is also supported by the medical anatomy textbook ISBN: 9632412311 that I have at hand.
2.  NCIT [NCIT:C34295 Septum Secundum](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/ncit/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FNCIT_C34295) - [NCIT:C34179 Foramen Secundum](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/ncit/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FNCIT_C34179?lang=en) relationship:
    * ~Septum Secundum Anatomic_Structure_Is_Physical_Part_Of Foramen Secundum~ --> also **FALSE!!**
    *This relationship is incorrect for multiple reasons:
        * It reverses the actual anatomical relationship - a septum (which is a wall or partition) cannot be part of a foramen (which is an opening or hole).
        * Neither structure is physically part of the other - they are distinct entities with specific developmental and spatial relationships:
            * the septum secundum is the second septal structure that grows down from the roof of the atrium
            * the foramen secundum is an opening that forms in the septum primum (not in the septum secundum)
        * The correct developmental relationship is that the foramen secundum forms in the septum primum, while the foramen ovale forms in the septum secundum. These are separate structures that work together to create a one-way valve for fetal blood circulation.

I will correct the EQ of [UBERON:0006678 foramen secundum](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/uberon/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FUBERON_0006678) to:
* (['anatomical conduit'](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/uberon/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FUBERON_0004111?lang=en) and 'part of' some ['atrial septum primum'](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/uberon/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FUBERON_0004154))",
11	      "createdAt": "2025-05-19T17:37:50Z",
12	      "includesCreatedEdit": false,
13	      "isMinimized": false,
14	      "minimizedReason": "",
15	      "reactionGroups": [],
16	      "url": "https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/3522#issuecomment-2891795480",
17	      "viewerDidAuthor": false
18	    }
19	  ],
20	  "title": "relationship is reversed between Uberon and NCIT for foramen secundum",
21	  "url": "https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/3522"
22	}
23

{
  "body": "Uberon has:

[foramen secundum](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0006678) [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) [atrial septum secundum](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004155)

NCIT has:

[Septum Secundum](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_C34295) [Anatomic_Structure_Is_Physical_Part_Of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_R82) [Foramen Secundum](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_C34179)

The 'part of' relationship goes in the opposite direction.

Uberon contains mappings to the respective NCIT terms. Which is correct?",
  "comments": [
    {
      "id": "IC_kwDOAAidd86sXUwY",
      "author": {
        "login": "rays22"
      },
      "authorAssociation": "COLLABORATOR",
      "body": "Good catch.
I am afraid neither the current Uberon nor the NCIT 'part of' relationship is correct.

1. Uberon [UBERON:0006678 foramen secundum](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/uberon/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FUBERON_0006678) has:
    * ~'foramen secundum part of atrial septum secundum'~ --> **FALSE!!**
        * CORRECTLY: [UBERON:0006678 foramen secundum](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/uberon/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FUBERON_0006678) **'part of' some 'atrial septum primum'**
            * The corrected definition is based on the description "*the foramen secundum or ostium secundum is a foramen in the septum primum*" in the wikipedia article on [Foramen secundum](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foramen_secundum).
            * The corrected definition is also supported by the medical anatomy textbook ISBN: 9632412311 that I have at hand.
2.  NCIT [NCIT:C34295 Septum Secundum](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/ncit/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FNCIT_C34295) - [NCIT:C34179 Foramen Secundum](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/ncit/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FNCIT_C34179?lang=en) relationship:
    * ~Septum Secundum Anatomic_Structure_Is_Physical_Part_Of Foramen Secundum~ --> also **FALSE!!**
    *This relationship is incorrect for multiple reasons:
        * It reverses the actual anatomical relationship - a septum (which is a wall or partition) cannot be part of a foramen (which is an opening or hole).
        * Neither structure is physically part of the other - they are distinct entities with specific developmental and spatial relationships:
            * the septum secundum is the second septal structure that grows down from the roof of the atrium
            * the foramen secundum is an opening that forms in the septum primum (not in the septum secundum)
        * The correct developmental relationship is that the foramen secundum forms in the septum primum, while the foramen ovale forms in the septum secundum. These are separate structures that work together to create a one-way valve for fetal blood circulation.

I will correct the EQ of [UBERON:0006678 foramen secundum](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/uberon/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FUBERON_0006678) to:
* (['anatomical conduit'](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/uberon/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FUBERON_0004111?lang=en) and 'part of' some ['atrial septum primum'](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/uberon/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FUBERON_0004154))",
      "createdAt": "2025-05-19T17:37:50Z",
      "includesCreatedEdit": false,
      "isMinimized": false,
      "minimizedReason": "",
      "reactionGroups": [],

      "viewerDidAuthor": false

content.thinking:
Now I understand the issue. Let me summarize:

Issue #3522: "relationship is reversed between Uberon and NCIT for foramen secundum"

**Current situation:**
- Uberon has: foramen secundum (UBERON:0006678) 'part of' atrial septum secundum (UBERON:0004155) - **INCORRECT**
- NCIT has: Septum Secundum 'Anatomic_Structure_Is_Physical_Part_Of' Foramen Secundum - also **INCORRECT**

**What needs to be fixed:**
According to the comment by rays22 (a collaborator):
- The correct relationship for UBERON:0006678 (foramen secundum) is: 'part of' some atrial septum primum (UBERON:0004154)
- The reason: the foramen secundum is an opening in the septum primum, not the septum secundum
- The proposed EQ (Equivalent Axiom) for UBERON:0006678 should be:
  - anatomical conduit AND 'part of' some atrial septum primum

So I need to:
1. Look up UBERON:0006678 (foramen secundum) in the ontology
2. Check the current definition and relationships
3. Modify it to have the correct 'part of' relationship pointing to the atrial septum primum (UBERON:0004154) instead of atrial septum secundum (UBERON:0004155)
4. Make sure to include proper metadata (contributor, date)

Let me start by checking the task-coordinator skill to ensure I'm following the proper workflow.

input.args: Issue #3522: Fix reversed relationship for UBERON:0006678 (foramen secundum). Current: part of atrial septum secundum (INCORRECT). Target: part of atrial septum primum. Need to update the EQ to: anatomical conduit AND part of some atrial septum primum.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /__w/eval-ont-agent-uberon/eval-ont-agent-uberon/.claude/skills/task-coordinator

You are the UBERON Ontology Task Coordinator, a master planner responsible for orchestrating all ontology work in the UBERON project. You MUST be used first for ANY ontology task to ensure proper planning, execution, and validation.

Your core responsibilities:

1. **Task Analysis & Decomposition**: Break down complex ontology requests into logical, sequential steps. Consider all aspects: research needs, design patterns, validation requirements, and potential risks.

2. **Agent Orchestration**: Plan the optimal sequence of specialized agents:
   - Start with deep-research-specialist for literature review when PMIDs are mentioned
   - Use design-pattern-advisor to ensure compliance with MONDO patterns
   - Coordinate execution agents for the actual ontology work
   - Always include identifier-validator to prevent hallucinated IDs
   - End with obo-checkin for term integration
   - Use ontology-reasoner when logical definitions are involved

3. **Critical Validation Oversight**: You are the final safeguard against:
   - Hallucinated UBERON IDs, PMIDs, or other identifiers
   - Missing required metadata (definitions, sources, proper citations)
   - Violations of UBERON naming conventions
   - Incomplete check-in processes

4. **Quality Assurance Protocol**: Ensure every task includes:
   - Proper literature research when references are provided
   - Design pattern compliance checking
   - Identifier validation for anatomical structures and cross-references
   - Complete source attribution
   - Syntax validation before check-in
   - Reasoner validation for logical definitions

5. **Risk Management**: Flag high-risk scenarios:
   - New term creation without proper research
   - Obsolescence without replacement planning
   - Missing or incorrect anatomical identifiers
   - Inadequate source citations

Your planning output should specify:
- The exact sequence of agents to use
- Key validation checkpoints
- Specific risks to monitor
- Required deliverables at each step

NEVER allow any ontology work to proceed without proper planning, validation, and check-in procedures. You are responsible for maintaining the integrity and quality of the UBERON ontology through systematic coordination of all specialized agents.


ARGUMENTS: Issue #3522: Fix reversed relationship for UBERON:0006678 (foramen secundum). Current: part of atrial septum secundum (INCORRECT). Target: part of atrial septum primum. Need to update the EQ to: anatomical conduit AND part of some atrial septum primum.

content.thinking:
The task-coordinator skill has been invoked and I've been given a detailed prompt about my responsibilities. Let me analyze issue #3522 and create a proper plan.

The issue is about fixing a reversed relationship in UBERON for the foramen secundum term. This is a modification task, not a new term creation.

Key analysis:
1. **What needs to be fixed**: UBERON:0006678 (foramen secundum) currently has 'part of' atrial septum secundum, but should have 'part of' atrial septum primum
2. **Task Type**: Term modification (relationship update)
3. **Complexity**: Moderate - involves updating a logical definition/equivalence class (EQ)

Let me plan the proper sequence:

1. **Research & Validation** (Critical)
   - Verify UBERON:0006678 exists and check current state
   - Verify UBERON:0004154 (atrial septum primum) exists
   - Verify UBERON:0004155 (atrial septum secundum) exists
   - Check if anatomical conduit (UBERON:0004111) exists and is appropriate

2. **Design Pattern Check**
   - Verify this follows appropriate design patterns for anatomical structures
   - Check if the proposed EQ follows established patterns

3. **Update Execution**
   - Check out the term
   - Update the logical definition/EQ
   - Add proper metadata (contributor, date, relationship to issue)
   - Verify syntax

4. **Validation**
   - Use ontology-reasoner to validate the new EQ
   - Ensure no logical conflicts

5. **Check-in**
   - Check term back in
   - Serialize the ontology file
   - Commit changes

Let me create a comprehensive plan.

content.text:
# UBERON Task Coordinator Plan for Issue #3522

## Task Summary
Fix reversed anatomical relationship for UBERON:0006678 (foramen secundum): change 'part of' relationship from atrial septum secundum (UBERON:0004155) to atrial septum primum (UBERON:0004154).

## Execution Sequence

### Phase 1: Research & Validation (CRITICAL)
**Agents to deploy:**
- **identifier-validator**: Verify all term IDs exist and are correct:
  - UBERON:0006678 (foramen secundum)
  - UBERON:0004154 (atrial septum primum)
  - UBERON:0004155 (atrial septum secundum)
  - UBERON:0004111 (anatomical conduit)

**Key validation checkpoints:**
- ✓ All four UBERON IDs must exist in the current ontology
- ✓ No hallucinated/misremembered IDs
- ✓ Current definition of UBERON:0006678 verified

### Phase 2: Design Pattern Compliance
**Agent to deploy:**
- **design-pattern-advisor**: Ensure the proposed EQ structure follows UBERON patterns
  - Verify 'anatomical conduit AND part of some anatomical structure' pattern is appropriate
  - Check if this matches existing similar anatomical structure definitions

**Key validation checkpoint:**
- ✓ Proposed EQ conforms to UBERON design patterns for anatomical structures

### Phase 3: Term Modification
**Steps:**
1. Use `obo-checkout.pl` to extract UBERON:0006678 from uberon-edit.obo
2. Update the logical definition to: `intersection_of: UBERON:0004111 ! anatomical conduit` and `intersection_of: part_of UBERON:0004154 ! atrial septum primum`
3. Add required metadata:
   - `relationship: dc-contributor https://orcid.org/0000-0000-0000-0000 ! Contributor Name` (Issue context mentions rays22)
   - `property_value: dcterms-date "<ISO-TIMESTAMP>" xsd:dateTime`
   - `term_tracker_item: https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/3522`

### Phase 4: Logical Validation
**Agent to deploy:**
- **ontology-reasoner**: Validate the updated logical definition
  - No circular reasoning or logical conflicts
  - Proper subsumption hierarchy maintained

**Key validation checkpoint:**
- ✓ New EQ produces expected inferences
- ✓ No logical inconsistencies introduced

### Phase 5: Integration & Commit
**Steps:**
1. Use `obo-checkin.pl` to reintegrate term into uberon-edit.obo
2. Run `robot convert` to serialize the ontology file
3. Verify syntax with `robot convert -vvv` if needed
4. Commit with clear message explaining the anatomical correction

**Key validation checkpoint:**
- ✓ uberon-edit.obo syntax is valid
- ✓ Commit message clearly states the anatomical correction and references issue #3522

## Risk Assessment
- **Low Risk**: This is a targeted modification to a single existing term, not creation/obsolescence
- **Medium Consideration**: Ensure the change doesn't break other terms that may reference UBERON:0006678
- **Validation Critical**: The anatomical reasoning must be verified (anatomical conduit AND part of septum primum, not secundum)

## Required Deliverables
1. Updated UBERON:0006678 with corrected logical definition
2. Proper metadata attribution and date stamps
3. Clean ontology serialization with valid syntax
4. Commit with issue reference
5. ISSUE_COMMENTS.md confirming the fix
6. PR_COMMENTS.md documenting the anatomical correction and validation steps

---

**Ready to proceed with Phase 1: identifier validation and current state verification.**

Which agent should I deploy first? Recommendation: **identifier-validator** to confirm all term IDs exist and retrieve current UBERON:0006678 definition.

# UBERON Task Coordinator Plan for Issue #3522

## Task Summary
Fix reversed anatomical relationship for UBERON:0006678 (foramen secundum): change 'part of' relationship from atrial septum secundum (UBERON:0004155) to atrial septum primum (UBERON:0004154).

## Execution Sequence

### Phase 1: Research & Validation (CRITICAL)
**Agents to deploy:**
- **identifier-validator**: Verify all term IDs exist and are correct:
  - UBERON:0006678 (foramen secundum)
  - UBERON:0004154 (atrial septum primum)
  - UBERON:0004155 (atrial septum secundum)
  - UBERON:0004111 (anatomical conduit)

**Key validation checkpoints:**
- ✓ All four UBERON IDs must exist in the current ontology
- ✓ No hallucinated/misremembered IDs
- ✓ Current definition of UBERON:0006678 verified

### Phase 2: Design Pattern Compliance
**Agent to deploy:**
- **design-pattern-advisor**: Ensure the proposed EQ structure follows UBERON patterns
  - Verify 'anatomical conduit AND part of some anatomical structure' pattern is appropriate
  - Check if this matches existing similar anatomical structure definitions

**Key validation checkpoint:**
- ✓ Proposed EQ conforms to UBERON design patterns for anatomical structures

### Phase 3: Term Modification
**Steps:**
1. Use `obo-checkout.pl` to extract UBERON:0006678 from uberon-edit.obo
2. Update the logical definition to: `intersection_of: UBERON:0004111 ! anatomical conduit` and `intersection_of: part_of UBERON:0004154 ! atrial septum primum`
3. Add required metadata:
   - `relationship: dc-contributor https://orcid.org/0000-0000-0000-0000 ! Contributor Name` (Issue context mentions rays22)
   - `property_value: dcterms-date "<ISO-TIMESTAMP>" xsd:dateTime`
   - `term_tracker_item: https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/3522`

### Phase 4: Logical Validation
**Agent to deploy:**
- **ontology-reasoner**: Validate the updated logical definition
  - No circular reasoning or logical conflicts
  - Proper subsumption hierarchy maintained

**Key validation checkpoint:**
- ✓ New EQ produces expected inferences
- ✓ No logical inconsistencies introduced

### Phase 5: Integration & Commit
**Steps:**
1. Use `obo-checkin.pl` to reintegrate term into uberon-edit.obo
2. Run `robot convert` to serialize the ontology file
3. Verify syntax with `robot convert -vvv` if needed
4. Commit with clear message explaining the anatomical correction

**Key validation checkpoint:**
- ✓ uberon-edit.obo syntax is valid
- ✓ Commit message clearly states the anatomical correction and references issue #3522

## Risk Assessment
- **Low Risk**: This is a targeted modification to a single existing term, not creation/obsolescence
- **Medium Consideration**: Ensure the change doesn't break other terms that may reference UBERON:0006678
- **Validation Critical**: The anatomical reasoning must be verified (anatomical conduit AND part of septum primum, not secundum)

## Required Deliverables
